title: Hash-based sync protocol with pull and push
purpose: Deliver the hash-based sync protocol that enables two haematite nodes to synchronise a shard by comparing root hashes and exchanging only the differing content-addressed nodes. This is the transport-layer foundation for multi-node haematite — merge logic and consistency modes build on it in DIST-002.
task: Implement the sync protocol: root hash exchange, simultaneous tree walk descending only into differing subtrees, and node transfer. Implement pull sync (target initiates, requests missing nodes) and push sync (source responds with requested nodes). Sync operates per-shard over beamr's existing distribution connections. Received nodes are applied to the target's local node store. Partial sync (crash mid-transfer) must not corrupt either node's state. Does not implement merge logic (DIST-002) or topology (DIST-003).

requirements.title: Sync module structure
requirements.spec: Create the sync module with mod.rs containing only pub mod declarations. Create protocol.rs, pull.rs, and push.rs as sub-modules.
acceptance: crates/haematite/src/sync/mod.rs exists with only pub mod and pub use statements
crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs exists
crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs exists
crates/haematite/src/sync/push.rs exists

files: crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:1-9 — crate root already declares `pub mod sync;` alongside other top-level public modules
crates/haematite/src/sync/mod.rs:1-7 — current sync root is declaration-only and includes future DIST-002/DIST-003 modules too
crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:1-1 — existing DIST-001 stub file
crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:1-1 — existing DIST-001 stub file
crates/haematite/src/sync/push.rs:1-1 — existing DIST-001 stub file
context: Acceptance says create files, but they already exist in this worktree; treat this as filling stubs rather than creating from scratch.
Module roots in this crate are intentionally small declaration/export files: compare `tree/mod.rs:1-11`, `branch/mod.rs:1-18`, and current `sync/mod.rs:1-7`.

`lib.rs` already exports `sync` publicly at line 5, so no crate-root change is needed unless new sync types should be re-exported at the crate root; if so, follow the grouped `pub use` style used at lines 14-35.

Do not remove `consistency`, `merge`, `scheduler`, or `topology` declarations just because this brief only implements DIST-001; design structure assigns those files to later briefs and their stubs already exist.
scout.approach: Keep `sync/mod.rs` as declarations plus any intentional `pub use` surface for DIST-001 types/functions. Implement public protocol types in the submodules, and re-export only stable user-facing items from `sync/mod.rs` if needed. Leave `lib.rs` alone unless tests require crate-root exports; if adding exports, group them similarly to existing `pub use tree::{...}` blocks and avoid putting implementation in module roots.
scout.notes: The brief wording says `mod.rs` should contain only `pub mod` and `pub use` statements; current file satisfies that. The costly mistake would be deleting future module declarations to make DIST-001 look minimal, which would conflict with the distribution design structure.

files_changed.note: Kept the sync root declaration/export-only and added stable DIST-001 public re-exports.
Filled the existing protocol stub with public sync types, root-exchange planning, errors, and missing-node discovery.
Filled the existing pull stub with target-side pull request/application helpers.
Filled the existing push stub with source-side pull-response helpers.
Private protocol implementation submodule for sync wire codec and beamr distribution adapter seam.
Private protocol test submodule extracted to keep protocol.rs under file-length conventions.

dev.how: The existing sync module/files were treated as stubs per scout guidance. `sync/mod.rs` contains only `pub mod` declarations and `pub use` re-exports, while implementation lives in `protocol.rs`, `pull.rs`, `push.rs`, and private protocol submodules; `lib.rs` already had `pub mod sync;` and was left unchanged.
dev.deviation: Added private implementation files under `crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/` to keep `protocol.rs` within the repository's file-length convention while preserving the requested public module structure. This does not change `sync/mod.rs` and does not add public top-level sync modules.

requirements.title: Root hash exchange
requirements.spec: WHEN sync is initiated for a shard, THE SYSTEM SHALL exchange root hashes between source and target. IF root hashes are identical, THE SYSTEM SHALL complete immediately with no node transfer. IF root hashes differ, THE SYSTEM SHALL proceed to the tree walk phase.
acceptance: Sync between two nodes with identical root hashes completes with zero node transfers
Sync between two nodes with different root hashes proceeds to tree walk
The root hash exchange uses beamr's distribution connection

files: crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:1-1 — target file is a stub; root-exchange/result types likely belong here
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:9-24 — `Hash` is a 32-byte copyable value with byte accessors for wire payloads
crates/haematite/src/branch/handle.rs:168-178 — branch handles expose per-shard current roots as `Option<Hash>`
crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:7-13 — `NodeStore` is the generic abstraction used throughout tree code
crates/haematite/Cargo.toml:10-16 — `beamr = "0.6.4"` is already a dependency; use it rather than adding transport dependencies
context: Use `Hash` directly in protocol structs; it is `Copy`, `Eq`, printable hex via `Display`, and serializable via `as_bytes`/`from_bytes`.
Existing APIs are generic over `S: NodeStore + ?Sized` and convert store errors into domain errors rather than exposing concrete store types.
The root for a specific shard can come from `BranchHandle::shard_current_root`, and unknown shard should be treated explicitly rather than silently using the primary root.

beamr integration seam in 0.6.4 is `beamr::distribution::ConnectionManager`, which exposes `get_connection`, `connect_node`, `register_control_frame_handler`, and `DistConnection::write_raw`; root exchange should be framed through that manager/connection, not `TcpStream`.
scout.approach: Define a small root-exchange request/response model in `protocol.rs`, including `ShardId`, local root, remote root, and a `SyncDecision`/`SyncPlan` that records whether transfer is needed and initializes `nodes_transferred = 0`. The pure decision function should immediately short-circuit when hashes match without touching a store. Add a transport-facing function or trait boundary that accepts/uses beamr distribution connection handles for sending encoded root exchange messages, keeping the protocol testable without live networking.
scout.notes: Root exchange does not require loading any nodes; tests should assert zero store reads and zero transfers for equal roots. Avoid inventing a custom TCP listener/client; all wire operations should be behind beamr `ConnectionManager`/`DistConnection::write_raw` or a narrow adapter around it.

files_changed.note: Added `RootExchange`, `RootExchangeRequest`, `RootExchangeResponse`, `SyncDecision`, `SyncPlan`, and `plan_sync`.
Added beamr-frame encoding/decoding and handler/sender seams for root exchange and other sync messages.
Added tests for identical-root short-circuit, differing-root tree-walk decision, and beamr frame round-trip.

dev.how: Sync planning is per shard and begins by comparing optional source/target root hashes. Identical roots return `AlreadySynced` with `nodes_transferred = 0` and no tree walk; differing roots return `WalkTrees`. Root request/response messages include shard id and roots, and the wire helper frames sync messages for beamr distribution control frames rather than creating sockets.
dev.deviation: The scout suggested accepting/using `ConnectionManager::connect_node` if a connection is absent. To avoid adding runtime ownership or hidden socket lifecycle in haematite, `send_sync_message_via_beamr` requires an existing beamr distribution connection from `ConnectionManager` and hands the encoded frame to a caller-provided writer closure; this still enforces the boundary that sync uses beamr distribution connections and never opens a separate TCP connection.

checklist.note: `RootExchangeRequest`, `RootExchangeResponse`, and `plan_sync` model root exchange for a specific `ShardId`.
`plan_sync` returns `AlreadySynced`, zero transfers, and zero tree walks when roots are identical; covered by unit test.

stories.note: Public root-exchange types expose per-shard local/remote roots and a sync decision for debugging comparisons.

acceptance.criterion: Sync between two nodes with identical root hashes completes with zero node transfers
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:114-119 selects AlreadySynced for equal roots; protocol.rs:157-163 starts a tree walk only when required; test sync::protocol::tests::identical_roots_short_circuit_without_tree_walk asserts tree_walks=0 and nodes_transferred=0 at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/tests.rs:76-87.

acceptance.criterion: Sync between two nodes with different root hashes proceeds to tree walk
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:114-119 selects WalkTrees for differing roots and protocol.rs:157-163 records tree_walks=1; test sync::protocol::tests::differing_roots_trigger_tree_walk asserts the WalkTrees decision at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/tests.rs:91-100.

acceptance.criterion: The root hash exchange uses beamr's distribution connection
acceptance.evidence: RootExchangeRequest/RootExchangeResponse carry shard/root data at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:168-205; SyncMessage encodes/decodes root request/response at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/wire.rs:41-52 and 82-92; typed send_root_exchange_request_via_beamr/send_root_exchange_response_via_beamr route through send_sync_message_via_beamr at wire.rs:170-202, which requires ConnectionManager::get_connection at wire.rs:154-167.

issues: Initial code exposed generic beamr frame helpers but not phase-specific root-exchange sends, weakening the evidence that root exchange itself used beamr distribution connections.
fixes: Added typed send_root_exchange_request_via_beamr and send_root_exchange_response_via_beamr helpers over the existing beamr ConnectionManager seam.

requirements.title: Differential tree walk
requirements.spec: WHEN root hashes differ, THE SYSTEM SHALL walk both trees simultaneously from root to leaves, descending only into subtrees where hashes differ. THE SYSTEM SHALL skip subtrees where both hashes match. THE SYSTEM SHALL identify nodes present in the source but absent in the target.
acceptance: The walk descends only into subtrees where hashes differ
Matching subtrees are skipped entirely
The walk identifies the minimal set of missing nodes
Two databases sharing 99% of nodes transfer only the differing 1%

files: crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:1-1 — implement missing-node walk here, probably as pure functions plus result structs
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:107-150 — internal nodes expose ordered `(separator, child_hash)` children and stable child hashes
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:152-180 — `Node` enum has `serialise`, `deserialise`, and `hash` for transfer/validation
crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs:47-96 — existing simultaneous tree diff shows equal-root early return and recursive hash-pruned walking
crates/haematite/tests/core003.rs:242-283 — tests demonstrate panic/counting stores to prove shared subtrees are skipped and mostly-shared trees read few nodes
context: Do not use `tree::diff` as-is for sync: it reports key/value diffs, not content-addressed nodes to transfer. Reuse its traversal ideas and helper logic conceptually.

For missing-node transfer, source and target may not share a physical `NodeStore`; target absence should be checked by hash, and source nodes should be read only when their hash is not already present on target or when needed to descend.

The minimal transfer set should include internal nodes required to connect to missing descendants as well as missing leaves; sending only leaves leaves the target unable to materialize the source root.
Existing tests use `CountingStore`/`PanicOnHashStore` patterns to prove equal/shared subtrees are not loaded; replicate that pattern for sync node discovery.
scout.approach: Implement a `find_missing_nodes(source_store, target_store, source_root, target_root)` style function returning ordered `Vec<NodeTransfer>` plus stats. First return empty if roots match. Otherwise recursively compare hashes: if the target store already has the source hash, skip that whole subtree; if the source hash is absent on target, load the source node, include it for transfer, and if it is internal decide whether to descend to collect child nodes or include a closure of all child nodes required for applying the root. When both sides have differing internal nodes, compare child hashes by separator/range like `tree::diff` so identical children are pruned. Keep the output deterministic by traversing children in stored order and de-duplicating hashes with a `BTreeSet`/`HashSet`.
scout.notes: The current `NodeStore` only stores/retrieves full `Node`, not raw bytes, so any transfer struct should carry `Hash` plus `Node` (or serialized bytes with hash verification before `put`). Shape divergence is tricky: source leaf vs target internal and vice versa must still produce all source nodes that target lacks, not a key diff.

files_changed.note: Implemented hash-pruned `find_missing_nodes` and transfer/stat types.
Added tests proving matching subtrees are skipped and mostly shared trees transfer only the changed leaf plus new root.

dev.how: `find_missing_nodes` compares roots, skips equal child hashes without loading them, probes target presence by source hash, descends deterministically through differing internal children, de-duplicates visited source hashes, and returns child-before-parent `NodeTransfer`s so missing descendants are available before parent/root nodes are applied. Stats report tree walks, skipped matching/existing subtrees, reads/probes, nodes, and bytes transferred.
dev.deviation: The scout mentioned comparing child ranges by separator/range like `tree::diff`; this implementation compares matching separator child slots for sync's content-addressed subtree transfer and falls back to transferring the full missing source subtree where shapes/separators diverge. That preserves correctness/minimality by hash availability for content-addressed node transfer without implementing key-diff merge semantics from DIST-002.

checklist.note: Differing roots trigger `find_missing_nodes`, which recursively descends only source children whose hashes are not equal to the corresponding target child and not already present on the target.
The transfer list contains only content-addressed source nodes absent from the target store, including required internal parent nodes.

stories.note: `SyncStats` reports `nodes_transferred` and `bytes_transferred` for sync magnitude debugging.

acceptance.criterion: The walk descends only into subtrees where hashes differ
acceptance.evidence: collect_missing_node returns immediately for equal target/source hashes at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:373-375, probes target presence before reading source nodes at protocol.rs:382-385, and recurses only over source internal children with target child summaries at protocol.rs:409-423; test sync::protocol::tests::tree_walk_skips_matching_children_and_transfers_missing_nodes_post_order asserts only the changed child and root are transferred at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/tests.rs:144-154.

acceptance.criterion: Matching subtrees are skipped entirely
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:373-375 increments matching_subtrees_skipped and returns without reading descendants; test sync::protocol::tests::tree_walk_skips_matching_children_and_transfers_missing_nodes_post_order asserts matching_subtrees_skipped=2 and counted source reads=2 at protocol/tests.rs:150-154.

acceptance.criterion: The walk identifies the minimal set of missing nodes
acceptance.evidence: Target presence by source hash is skipped at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:382-385; transfers are appended children-before-parent only after missing descendants are processed at protocol.rs:426-428; tests assert transfer hashes exactly [source_middle_hash, source_root] at protocol/tests.rs:144-153 and [source_only_hash, source_root] at crates/haematite/src/sync/push.rs:139-147.

acceptance.criterion: Two databases sharing 99% of nodes transfer only the differing 1%
acceptance.evidence: test sync::protocol::tests::mostly_shared_trees_transfer_only_differing_leaf_and_new_root builds 100 children with one differing child and asserts 99 matching subtrees skipped and only 2 necessary content-addressed nodes transferred at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/tests.rs:158-207.

issues: The initial source-side walk was tied to an in-process target NodeStore, leaving remote tree-walk semantics under-specified.
fixes: Introduced TargetNodeSummary/TargetNodeReader so the source can walk using remote target structural summaries; added sync::protocol::tests::tree_walk_can_use_remote_target_node_summaries at protocol/tests.rs:211-255.

requirements.title: Pull sync — target-initiated
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement pull sync where the target initiates, sends its root hash, and receives missing nodes from the source. THE SYSTEM SHALL apply received nodes to the target's local node store.
acceptance: The target initiates the sync by sending its root hash
The target receives missing nodes from the source
Received nodes are stored in the target's node store
After pull sync, the target has all nodes present in the source

files: crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:1-1 — target-initiated pull stub to implement
crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:1-1 — use root exchange/missing-node transfer types from here instead of duplicating protocol logic
crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:7-13 — `NodeStore::put` returns the content hash; use it to apply received nodes generically
crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:77-90 — disk `put` is idempotent and no-ops if a node file already exists
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:175-180 — received node hash can be recomputed before accepting/applying
context: Pull is target-initiated: it should send target shard/root information to the source, receive missing nodes, and apply them to the target store.
The store contract is content-addressed: `put(&Node)` computes the hash, so pull code should verify that the received/declared hash equals `node.hash()` or the returned hash.
Applying received nodes one-by-one is consistent with partial sync safety; there is no existing transaction mechanism around `NodeStore`, and the disk store is already atomic per node.
Do not attempt to compute a merged root or resolve conflicts here; design says sync-time merge is DIST-002.
scout.approach: Implement a public pull function that accepts the target store mutably, target shard/root, source identity/connection or an abstract sync client, and returns `SyncStats`/`PullResult`. It should send a pull request containing shard id and target root, receive a source root plus missing `NodeTransfer`s, then for each transfer verify hash integrity and `put` into the local `NodeStore`. For equal roots, return success with zero transfers. For different roots, after applying nodes, report the source root now available locally, but do not mutate branch roots or merge state in DIST-001.
scout.notes: Because `NodeStore::put` takes `&mut self`, pull application is naturally serialized; per-shard independence should come from not using any global sync lock. If tests use `MemoryStore`, they can assert `target.get(&source_root).is_some()` after pull.

files_changed.note: Implemented target-initiated pull request creation, push-response application, local pull helper, and pull tests.
Re-exported pull API.

dev.how: The target creates a `PullRequest` containing shard id and target root, receives a `PushResponse`, validates shard/root consistency, verifies every received node's declared hash against `node.hash()` and the store-returned hash, then writes each node into the target `NodeStore`. `pull_from_source` composes the same push-response logic for local tests and returns a `PullResult` with the source root now available in the target store; it does not mutate branch roots or merge state.
dev.deviation: No live remote client abstraction was introduced beyond protocol messages and beamr framing because the crate currently has no haematite-side request/response runtime. The implemented API provides the target-side request/application path and a local source helper for deterministic tests; actual network orchestration can layer over `SyncMessage`/beamr in later integration work without changing sync semantics.

checklist.note: `apply_push_response` validates and stores received nodes into the target `NodeStore`; tests assert source nodes/root are present afterward.

stories.note: The pull path can hydrate a new target node's store with all source-root nodes for one shard without manual copying.
Content-addressed pull application provides the storage-level replication primitive needed to copy workflow event-history tree nodes across nodes.

acceptance.criterion: The target initiates the sync by sending its root hash
acceptance.evidence: PullRequest carries shard_id and target_root at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:207-226; create_pull_request constructs it at crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:17-21; send_pull_request_via_beamr sends it through beamr at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/wire.rs:204-219.

acceptance.criterion: The target receives missing nodes from the source
acceptance.evidence: PushResponse contains transfers at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:262-270; apply_push_response iterates over response.transfers at crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:49-53; test sync::pull::tests::target_initiated_pull_applies_received_nodes exercises the end-to-end local pull helper at pull.rs:197-211.

acceptance.criterion: Received nodes are stored in the target's node store
acceptance.evidence: apply_transfer validates transfer.node.hash() and writes via target_store.put at crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:127-153.

acceptance.criterion: After pull sync, the target has all nodes present in the source
acceptance.evidence: test sync::pull::tests::target_initiated_pull_applies_received_nodes asserts target has source_root, source_left_hash, and source_right_hash after pull at crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:205-211.

issues: Pull had local application logic, but remote target-side target-node responses were missing for the source to compute deltas without local target-store access.
fixes: Added TargetNodeRequest/TargetNodeResponse and TargetNodeResponse::from_store at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/target.rs:41-93, plus target-node beamr encoding/sends in wire.rs:59-69, 98-106, and 238-270.

requirements.title: Push sync — source responds
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement push sync where the source responds to pull requests by walking its tree and sending requested nodes. THE SYSTEM SHALL send only nodes the target does not have.
acceptance: The source responds to a pull request with missing nodes
The source sends only nodes not present in the target
The source does not need to track target state — hash comparison determines the delta

files: crates/haematite/src/sync/push.rs:1-1 — source response stub to implement
crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:1-1 — central missing-node walker should be called from push responses
crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:7-13 — source and target presence checks should use `NodeStore::get` generically
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:118-149 — internal child hashes are the structural basis for sending only missing subtrees
docs/design/distribution/design.json:4-6 — design states source does not track target state; hash comparison determines delta
context: Push here means source responds to a pull request; it is not a scheduled outbound push/topology feature.

The source must not retain per-target replication state. All needed target knowledge should be carried in the pull request: shard id, target root hash, and/or target-has information discovered during tree walk.
Sending only nodes target lacks requires either probing target availability through protocol messages or using the target root comparison recursively; do not blindly stream the source tree.
Keep push response side-effect-free on the source store: it reads nodes and writes to the beamr connection only.
scout.approach: Create a `handle_pull_request`/`build_push_response` in `push.rs` that receives a `PullRequest { shard_id, target_root }`, source root, and source store, then invokes `protocol.rs` missing-node discovery. Return/send `PushResponse { shard_id, source_root, transfers, stats }`. The response should include no nodes when roots match, and for differing roots include exactly the protocol-computed missing transfers without caching target-specific state on the source.
scout.notes: A naive implementation may only compare root hashes and then send the entire source tree; that violates CN1. Another trap is implementing an active 'push to remote' workflow, which belongs to topology/scheduling later, not this source-response requirement.

files_changed.note: Implemented source-side pull response generation with tests.
Re-exported push API.

dev.how: `build_push_response` and `build_push_response_for_shard` accept a pull request/source root plus source and target `NodeStore` views, call the central hash-pruned missing-node discovery, and return a `PushResponse` containing no nodes for equal roots or exactly the missing source nodes for differing roots. The source function is read-only and carries no per-target replication state beyond the request/root comparison inputs.
dev.deviation: No active outbound topology/scheduled push workflow was implemented; this follows the scout note that R5's 'push' is the source response to a pull request, not DIST-003 topology/scheduling.

checklist.note: The source-side helper responds to `PullRequest` by walking the source/target tree hashes and returning the requested missing nodes.

stories.note: The push-response primitive enables eventually consistent read replicas by sending only content-addressed deltas to a pulling target.

acceptance.criterion: The source responds to a pull request with missing nodes
acceptance.evidence: build_push_response accepts PullRequest and source_root then delegates to build_push_response_for_shard at crates/haematite/src/sync/push.rs:16-33; build_push_response_for_shard calls find_missing_nodes and returns PushResponse at push.rs:38-62.

acceptance.criterion: The source sends only nodes not present in the target
acceptance.evidence: find_missing_nodes skips any source hash already present in the target reader at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:382-385; test sync::push::tests::push_response_sends_only_nodes_missing_from_target asserts shared_hash is not transferred and only source_only_hash plus source_root are sent at crates/haematite/src/sync/push.rs:133-147.

acceptance.criterion: The source does not need to track target state — hash comparison determines the delta
acceptance.evidence: push.rs:14-15 documents target_nodes can be a local store or remote TargetNodeRequest/TargetNodeResponse reader; build_push_response_for_shard requires only TargetNodeReader at push.rs:38-48; TargetNodeReader describes remote target reads without source-owned state at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/target.rs:96-104.

issues: The initial push API required a direct &T: NodeStore for the target, which is not available to a real remote source node.
fixes: Generalized build_push_response/build_push_response_for_shard to TargetNodeReader and added remote target summary test coverage.

requirements.title: Per-shard sync over beamr distribution
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL sync per-shard: each shard's tree syncs independently. THE SYSTEM SHALL route sync messages through beamr's existing distribution connection manager. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT create separate TCP connections for sync.
acceptance: Sync on shard 5 does not affect or block shards 0-4
Sync messages are sent via beamr distribution connections
No separate TCP connections are created for sync

files: crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:1-1 — protocol message structs should carry `ShardId` and abstract over beamr connection use
crates/haematite/src/branch/handle.rs:11-17 — `ShardId` is currently `usize` in branch handle until shard router defines a crate-wide type
crates/haematite/src/branch/handle.rs:162-188 — branch handle exposes contains/current-root/shard-id iteration for per-shard APIs
crates/haematite/Cargo.toml:10-16 — beamr is already declared; do not add networking crates
docs/design/distribution/design.json:62-87 — design assigns DIST-001 protocol/pull/push and reserves merge/consistency/topology for later files
context: Per-shard sync means every request/response must include a shard id and operate on one root pair only; do not iterate all shards inside a single sync call unless a caller explicitly does so above this layer.
`ShardId` is currently exported from `branch::handle` and re-exported from crate root; reuse it to avoid a competing type alias in sync.
beamr 0.6.4 provides `ConnectionManager`/`DistConnection`; it already owns TCP lifecycle and frame reading/writing. Sync code should encode payloads and send through these handles only.
No repository code currently wraps beamr distribution for haematite messages, so a small adapter/trait in `protocol.rs` may be needed for tests and to avoid coupling all logic to async beamr details.
scout.approach: Define sync message types with `shard_id: ShardId` and keep all functions scoped to a single `(shard_id, source_root, target_root)` tuple. Introduce a narrow `SyncTransport`/beamr adapter that can send/receive framed sync messages via `ConnectionManager::get_connection` and `DistConnection::write_raw`, and register a control-frame handler for inbound sync frames if needed. Do not create or bind sockets; if a connection is absent, either call beamr `connect_node` through the manager or return a transport error according to the chosen API.
scout.notes: The scout found no haematite-side beamr integration beyond the dependency, so the dev may need to inspect beamr docs/source while implementing. Keep transport tests injectable/mocked, because live beamr tests will otherwise be brittle and may require async runtime setup.

files_changed.note: All protocol request/response/result types carry `ShardId` and all discovery functions operate on one shard/root pair.
Implemented sync message encoding/decoding, beamr control-frame wrapping, handler registration, and existing-connection send seam.
Re-exported beamr framing/handler/sender helpers.

dev.how: Every sync message includes `ShardId`, and all public operations are scoped to a single `(shard_id, source_root, target_root)` rather than iterating or locking all shards. Wire helpers encode sync messages into beamr distribution control frames, register a beamr control-frame handler through `ConnectionManager::register_control_frame_handler`, and send via an existing `ConnectionManager`/`DistConnection` seam. No TCP listener/client/socket code was added.
dev.deviation: As in R2, the sender seam deliberately does not call `connect_node` itself; it requires beamr to own connection establishment beforehand and fails with `TransportConnectionUnavailable` if the existing distribution connection is absent. This is stricter than the scout's optional connect-on-missing idea and better preserves the 'no separate TCP connections' boundary in this crate layer.

checklist.note: All sync functions and messages carry one `ShardId` and operate on one shard's roots only.
Sync message framing/handler/sender APIs use beamr `ConnectionManager`/`DistConnection`; no non-beamr networking APIs were added.

stories.note: Per-shard, idempotent content-addressed transfers make failed sync attempts retryable without affecting unrelated shards.

acceptance.criterion: Sync on shard 5 does not affect or block shards 0-4
acceptance.evidence: All sync protocol operations are scoped to one ShardId: RootExchange at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:103-110, PullRequest at protocol.rs:207-212, PushResponse at protocol.rs:262-270, and find_missing_nodes arguments at protocol.rs:308-314; tests exercise shard 5 without iterating other shards, e.g. protocol/tests.rs:136-142 and pull.rs:197-203.

acceptance.criterion: Sync messages are sent via beamr distribution connections
acceptance.evidence: wire.rs imports beamr ConnectionManager/DistConnection at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/wire.rs:3-5; send_sync_message_via_beamr obtains an existing connection and writes a beamr sync frame at wire.rs:148-167; typed sends cover root, pull, push, and target-node messages at wire.rs:170-270; register_beamr_sync_handler registers with ConnectionManager at wire.rs:272-281.

acceptance.criterion: No separate TCP connections are created for sync
acceptance.evidence: send_sync_message_via_beamr explicitly uses manager.get_connection and returns TransportConnectionUnavailable when absent at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol/wire.rs:154-167, with no connect/listen/bind calls in sync code; final forbidden-networking check `rg -n "Tcp|Udp|Socket|std::net|tokio::net|connect_node|connect\(|bind\(|listen\(" crates/haematite/src/sync/**/*.rs crates/haematite/src/sync/*.rs` produced no matches.

issues: Beamr integration was initially only a generic frame seam; adversarial review required typed beamr-routed messages for the actual protocol phases.
fixes: Added typed beamr send helpers for root exchange, pull requests, push responses, target-node requests, and target-node responses; no custom networking was added.

requirements.title: Partial sync safety
requirements.spec: IF sync fails (network partition, node crash mid-transfer), THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT corrupt either node's local state. THE SYSTEM SHALL leave both nodes in a consistent state that can be retried.
acceptance: A crash after receiving 50% of missing nodes leaves the target with those 50% stored correctly
The target's existing data is not modified during sync
A retry after partial sync succeeds without re-sending already-received nodes

files: crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:1-1 — partial application/retry behavior belongs in pull receive/apply path
crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:77-90 — `DiskStore::write_node` skips existing nodes and caches them, making retries idempotent
crates/haematite/src/store/disk.rs:220-240 — disk writes use temp files, fsync, and `persist_noclobber`, so per-node writes are crash-safe
crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:31-35 — memory `put` overwrites by same content hash, also idempotent for retry tests
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:166-180 — deserialisation/hash validation prevents corrupt node payloads from being accepted
context: Partial sync safety is already aligned with content-addressed storage: adding nodes under their hashes does not mutate existing tree roots or WAL state.
The target's existing data should not be modified during DIST-001; do not update branch handles, committed shard roots, or merge state after a partial receive.
Retry can avoid re-sending already received nodes if missing-node discovery checks target `NodeStore::get(source_hash)` before requesting/sending a subtree.
Transport failure should return an error with counts/state, not roll back successfully written nodes.
scout.approach: In `pull.rs`, apply each received node independently: verify hash, call `NodeStore::put`, increment stats only after success, and return immediately on transport/store/hash errors. Do not stage changes that require rollback and do not delete/overwrite unrelated nodes. Ensure retry uses the same protocol discovery against the now-partially-populated target store, so already stored hashes are skipped and only remaining nodes are requested. Add tests with a fake receiver that fails after N transfers and assert the first N nodes are retrievable, pre-existing nodes remain, and retry transfers fewer nodes before reaching the source root.
scout.notes: The subtle issue is root visibility: after 50% of nodes arrive, the source root may still be absent or may point to missing children. That is okay for DIST-001 as long as stored nodes are valid and no committed target root is advanced until later merge/root-commit logic.

files_changed.note: Implemented independent node validation/application and prefix-apply partial-sync test helper.
Missing-node discovery skips hashes already present on target, enabling retries to avoid already received nodes.

dev.how: Pull application writes each validated `NodeTransfer` independently and increments stats only after a successful put. It never mutates branch roots, WAL state, merge state, or unrelated nodes. `apply_push_response_prefix` models a crash after a prefix of transfers, and retrying `pull_from_source` recomputes missing nodes against the partially populated target so already stored content-addressed nodes are skipped.
dev.deviation: No rollback/transaction layer was added; the implementation follows the scouted content-addressed/idempotent per-node write approach and deliberately leaves partial valid nodes in place for retry.

checklist.note: Partial-apply tests verify stored prefix nodes remain retrievable, existing target data remains retrievable, and retry sends only the remaining missing node(s).

stories.note: The target remains consistent after partial application and retry completes without re-sending already received hashes.

acceptance.criterion: A crash after receiving 50% of missing nodes leaves the target with those 50% stored correctly
acceptance.evidence: test sync::pull::tests::partial_apply_is_safe_and_retry_skips_already_received_nodes builds a two-node response, applies prefix limit 1, and asserts one node was stored while source_root remains absent at crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:253-258.

acceptance.criterion: The target's existing data is not modified during sync
acceptance.evidence: apply_transfer only validates and puts received content-addressed nodes at crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:127-153; partial-sync test asserts target_root and target_right_hash remain present after partial apply and retry at pull.rs:259-260 and 273-274.

acceptance.criterion: A retry after partial sync succeeds without re-sending already-received nodes
acceptance.evidence: find_missing_nodes skips target-present source hashes at crates/haematite/src/sync/protocol.rs:382-385; partial-sync test retries and asserts retry.stats.nodes_transferred == 1 after the first of two transfers was already applied at crates/haematite/src/sync/pull.rs:262-270.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement sync-time merge or conflict resolution — that is DIST-002
SHALL NOT implement consistency modes (eventual/strong) — that is DIST-002
SHALL NOT implement topology or sync scheduling — that is DIST-003
SHALL NOT create separate TCP connections — all sync uses beamr distribution

cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests
cargo clippy -p haematite produces no errors
grep -r 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/haematite/src/sync/ --include='*.rs' -l finds no non-test files
No file in crates/haematite/src/sync/ exceeds 500 lines
